subject: Integrating prometheus with Policy components
commit_message:
Integrating prometheus with Policy components

With this change, all PF components can export prometheus metrics
by default. The prometheus metrics servlet will be running on
/metrics servletPath..
If the metrics shouldn't be exported, just add a flag
    "promethus": false
in the restServerParameters of default configuration file.
This brings up prometheus servlet on /metrics servletPath.

Also as part of standard servlet support, "servletUriPath" and
"servletClass" are 2 other fields added to RestServerParameters
which can be used to add standard servlets. This can be revisited later
if needed.
Basically, any servlet can be added by passing these fields, for
example, to add prometheus metrics servlet on /test/metrics,
add the below to restServerParameters:
    "servletUriPath": "/test/metrics",
    "servletClass": "io.prometheus.client.exporter.MetricsServlet"

In addition, we can later go to individual components and add more
metrics, say for e.g.,
deployedPoliciesCounter/undeployedPoliciesCounter etc on PAP,
executedEvents/failedEvents etc on PDP and so on.
This will look something like below, for e.g. in policy-pap component:
 io.prometheus.client.Counter counter = Counter.build()
  .name("policies_deployed_total")
  .help("Number of policies deployed.").register()
Whenever a policy is deployed, just call
 counter.inc()

Usage of code like above in the individual component will expose
such data as well as part of the exposed metrics.

Change-Id: Id667f27b15c012398421ba657b5324cc1d82cf1f
Issue-ID: POLICY-3524
Signed-off-by: a.sreekumar <ajith.sreekumar@bell.ca>
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>
Signed-off-by: a.sreekumar <ajith.sreekumar@bell.ca>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-common-master-verify-java/1033/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-common-master-verify-java/1033

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

This is just a draft review, but I would like to get feedback from everyone about this. So, making this review ACTIVE.

With this change, all PF components can export default prometheus metrics like CPU/memory etc.

In addition, we can later go to individual components and add more metrics, say for e.g., deployedPoliciesCounter/undeployedPoliciesCounter etc on PAP, executedEvents/failedEvents etc on PDP and so on.
This will look something like below, for e.g. in policy-pap component:
io.prometheus.client.Counter counter = Counter.build()
        .name("policies_deployed_total")
        .help("Number of policies deployed.").register()

Whenever a policy is deployed, just call 
counter.inc()

Usage of the above code will expose this counter data as well as part of the exposed metrics.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-common-master-verify-java/1034/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-common-master-verify-java/1034

Patch Set 2:

(4 comments)

Thanks Ajith, this is great.   Since the Prometheus servlet for measurements is just a plain servlet. 
  I am thinking may be better to keep the http server endpoint agnostic of which type of servlet it is.   I thought it was supporting already out of the box but may be not.  It currently supports servlet filters (see.  JettyServletServer.addFilter - that AuthorizationFilter uses) and jax-rs endpoints.    I think adding generic servlet support if already not there would be great, this would be similar to the JettyServletServer.addFilter generic method.    This would be out of the box support for using the prometheus MetricServlet.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> (4 comments)
> 
> Thanks Ajith, this is great.   Since the Prometheus servlet for measurements is just a plain servlet. 
>   I am thinking may be better to keep the http server endpoint agnostic of which type of servlet it is.   I thought it was supporting already out of the box but may be not.  It currently supports servlet filters (see.  JettyServletServer.addFilter - that AuthorizationFilter uses) and jax-rs endpoints.    I think adding generic servlet support if already not there would be great, this would be similar to the JettyServletServer.addFilter generic method.    This would be out of the box support for using the prometheus MetricServlet.

Agree with your comments Jorge.
But I am not sure if we can make it generic to that level. Otherwise we will have to pass the Servletpath (/metrics in this case) and ServletClass (io.prometheus.client.exporter.MetricsServlet) from each component. If this should be generic enough to support multiple cases, then a list of <Servletpath, ServletClass> pair will have to be provided from each component. Should we go this way! It might be a little overkill. If atleast we go ahead with the new Servlet as one for Metrics, then we can go with "/metrics" and use whichever provider class of the metricsServlet the respective component passes.

Another option will be to go to each component and  write a jaxrs endpoint for collecting metrics. This doesn't seem to the preferred option though. Preferred option is to use MetricsServlet AFAIK.

I think I need to play if you don't mind with your review to understand better.  In the drools side, which was the seed code for the policy-endpoints in common, the factory goes by the properties, etc .. and there usually more 2 or more servers running, we would also want the capability to support the metrics server into its own server with nothing else just based on how somebody wants to configure it.
So, question, with the code that you provided, will it bring up a metrics server that I can take a look at?   what would be a sample curl request that I could feed it to test its working?

If I am correct, it won't create a new metric server, rather add the servlet provided to the existing server during startup. Example- for pap, we only have one rest server that comes up when pap component starts up. So it will just add this metric servlet with path /metrics to the same rest server which is serving /policy/pap/v1/healthcheck and other pap api's.
May be Ajith can confirm.

Patch Set 7: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-common-master-verify-java/1037/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-common-master-verify-java/1037

Patch Set 7:

I just uploaded a patch to this CI, Ajith, take a look at patch 7.   Best way to look at it is to look the junits that introduces several configuration possibilities.  I think these changes give a lot of flexibility to the individual component to set it up simply as they wish.  It provides generic support for non-JAX-RS servlets which is reused by the static server flavor.  It needs a little work on the RestServer which follows the original properties configuration that we use in drools, but I think if you want to keep it simple, just a boolean to enable the prometheus server will simply do it.   In any case, feel free to modify, or discard the code as you want.

Patch Set 8: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-common-master-verify-java/1038/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-common-master-verify-java/1038

Patch Set 8:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> I just uploaded a patch to this CI, Ajith, take a look at patch 7.   Best way to look at it is to look the junits that introduces several configuration possibilities.  I think these changes give a lot of flexibility to the individual component to set it up simply as they wish.  It provides generic support for non-JAX-RS servlets which is reused by the static server flavor.  It needs a little work on the RestServer which follows the original properties configuration that we use in drools, but I think if you want to keep it simple, just a boolean to enable the prometheus server will simply do it.   In any case, feel free to modify, or discard the code as you want.

I see that you cleaned it up, especially adding the servlet only once. I was planning to do such things once the main changes are finalized. Anyway that is cool, Thanks.
I think we don't need an extra way to initialize prometheus metrics specifically in policy-common when we support the initializing Servlets in a generic way with servletPath. So, removed such changes in patchset 8.
But my main question is, as per patchset6 I did, the way to add any servlet was by specifying them in the constructor from any component that uses it.
For example to add prometheus metrics servlet in policy-api:
if (params.isExportMetrics()) {
  restServer = new RestServer(params, AafApiFilter.class,
      Map.of("/metrics", MetricsServlet.class),
      ApiRestController.class);
}
where exportMetrics will be a boolean in the component configuration file.

With the latest changes of moving the servletPath and servletClass to properties,, how are we initializing new Servlets? We should add another entry for these details in restServerParameters of components. Otherwise, again we have to go as I mentioned above as per patchset6 (in which case some of the changes related to properties after patch6 may be removed)

> For example to add prometheus metrics servlet in policy-api:

>   restServer = new RestServer(params, AafApiFilter.class,
>       Map.of("/metrics", MetricsServlet.class),

> With the latest changes of moving the servletPath and servletClass to properties,, how are we initializing new Servlets? We should add another entry for these details in restServerParameters of components. Otherwise, again we have to go as I mentioned above as per patchset6 (in which case some of the changes related to properties after patch6 may be removed)

Oops, I see your question, I forgot to commit all my changes the other day, let me merge them, but yes, there is a prometheus flag equivalent (similar to aaf).   Let me submit them, and will make more sense by previous comment and this one.

Patch Set 8:

Oh, Ajith, I think you deleted some of my changes (patch 7) with your last patch (patch 8), not sure if purposefully, in any case patch 7 is that's what I described with my comments, the idea was to model after aaf (which adds a filter blindly with a boolean configuration flag), and allow for a generic way too that allows provision of any servlet (which can be used for the MetricsServlet), and allow for standalone servers with nothing else but the metrics server.   My thinking is that would cover most of the scenarios that one would want to cover.  I think this aligns too with the endpoints http server design.  In theory, JettyJerseyServlet should be touched minimally as it is concerned with integrating its own jersey servlet, and only used for jax-rs (rest) stuff and swagger (which is for jax-rs too). Generic servlet work should go in the parent class JettyServletServer and that is where  filters, auth, and generic servlet functionality is dealt with, just some background info.   
In any case, let me know your thoughts and we can discuss further whatever we are not aligned.
